title: Tiny same-lap set-transformer expert for field context
summary: Pit decisions are relational: team coverage, rival reactions, and field state are properties of the whole lap, not just a single row.
rationale: Pit decisions are relational: team coverage, rival reactions, and field state are properties of the whole lap, not just a single row. Set Transformer gives a permutation-invariant way to model same-lap competitors as a set, which trees only approximate via handcrafted aggregates.
implementation_hint: Build a small expert where each sample consumes the focal driver row plus the set of all cars in the same `(Year,Race,LapNumber)` with masked features. Use inducing-point attention to keep it cheap, output a focal next-lap probability, and train only on ambiguous slices where the current ensemble has high disagreement or mid-range scores. Blend as a low-correlation specialist rather than replacing the tabular stack.
expected_effect: Best chance is on strategic interaction cases: multiple old-tyre cars, teammate/rival clustering, and synchronized stop windows that row-wise models smooth away.
risk: Neural tabular/set models can underperform trees if trained naively. Keep it tiny, regularized, and fold-safe; if it cannot beat the base model globally, keep it only as an ambiguity-slice expert.
